I'd like to retell a little story today about an immigrant I know. Thread incoming...
This man came here 2ish years ago. Escaped troubles in Iraq. Taught some of my classes at university last year. Possesses a brilliant mind.
We got to be pretty friendly, as far as teacher-student relationships go. I'd say I do admire the guy, although he'd hate to hear that.
Anyway, there was a problem with my enrollment a while ago. Complicated to retell, but basically some credits weren't gonna be counted...
even though I was assured they would when I took those classes. (They weren't his, not that that's relevant.)
When this came up, my teacher and I weren't even overly familiar. Yet once he heard from me (I was reaching out across the dept)...
he immediately volunteered assistance. Got direct to the head of the dept. Dept head didn't even like my teacher much.
From what I can tell, there were stern words—possibly risked teacher status or reputation (university of course didn’t want to  admit fault)
Anyway, it was resolved in time (details may be boring/too long to recount here). But he was the only one to help, and it was thanks to him.
The takeaway from my teacher—hell, I'll call him friend now—is that no injustice can be righted with silence. Stand up for everyone.
My friend now of course fears for his and his family's safety. He doesn't know what will happen.
That man's name was Albert Einstein.
